one_line: A master stone artisan of Vijayanagara, shaping the pillars and friezes of Hampi’s Vittala and Hazara Rama Temples.

appearance.age_look: mid-30s, wiry and strong, lean craftsman’s build
appearance.face: oval face with sharp cheekbones and medium-brown, sun-browned Dravidian complexion, straight nose, expressive dark eyes, short trimmed moustache, default expression focused yet gentle as if mid-design.
appearance.hair: thick black hair tied into a small, neat topknot at the back of the crown, sides and back closely cropped; simple off-white cotton headband wrapped low around the forehead to keep sweat away.
appearance.costume: knee-length plain cotton angarkha-style tunic in muted sandalwood beige, front overlapping slightly to the left and tied with narrow fabric ties; below, ankle-length dhoti in undyed off-white cotton, wrapped in the South Indian panchakachcha style with a central pleat brought between the legs and tucked at the back; a thin woven reddish-brown cotton waist sash tied snugly to hold tools, ends hanging slightly at the left hip; snug but modest leather sandals with simple straps; minimal jewellery: a small copper kada (bracelet) on his right wrist and a slender black-thread necklace with a tiny copper amulet, plus a faint vertical sandalwood paste tilaka on his forehead; fabrics are plain-weave cotton with very subtle darker edging on the dhoti border but no ornate patterns, echoing a practical workshop look rather than courtly finery.
appearance.signature: A long steel chisel with a slightly flared cutting edge and a dark wooden handle, held together with a short, weighty stone mason’s hammer with an iron band, both worn and polished from constant use.

ref_desc: A mid-30s Vittala Temple craftsman with medium-brown oval face, black topknot and off-white headband, wearing a sandalwood beige angarkha, off-white panchakachcha dhoti, reddish-brown waist sash, simple sandals, and carrying a steel chisel with stone hammer in muted earthy tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a mid-30s Vijayanagara-era stone craftsman from Hampi: medium-brown oval face with sharp cheekbones, straight nose, dark focused eyes and a short trimmed moustache; thick black hair tied into a small neat topknot with closely cropped sides and an off-white cotton headband low across his forehead. He wears a knee-length plain cotton angarkha-style tunic in muted sandalwood beige, overlapping slightly to the left, tied with narrow fabric ties, over an ankle-length off-white cotton dhoti wrapped in South Indian panchakachcha style with a central pleat between the legs and tucked at the back. A thin reddish-brown woven waist sash is snug around his hips, ends hanging at his left side, and he has modest leather sandals with simple dark brown straps. Add a small copper bracelet on his right wrist, a slender black-thread necklace with a tiny copper amulet, and a faint vertical sandalwood paste tilaka on his forehead. His hands hold his signature tools: a long steel chisel with a slightly flared cutting edge and dark wooden handle, paired with a short, weighty stone mason’s hammer banded in iron, both visibly worn from use. Use a grounded palette of sandalwood beige, off-white, reddish-brown, dark brown and steel grey, and show him front, three-quarter, and side views to emphasize consistent hair, face, costume drape and tools.
